verdict_summary: Webcam fallback is VIABLE only with GazeRecorder-class tools (~1 deg, 30 Hz). WebGazer.js alone is NOT-VIABLE for OKN-based covert switch detection in the Boly bistable protocol — its 4 deg spatial error and 20 Hz mean rate fall below the OKN slow-phase velocity-vector requirement.

WebGazer.js.alternate_use: VIABLE for coarse 2x2 quadrant gaze (overt-report supplement only), NOT covert OKN switch detection
WebGazer.js.reason: 4 deg accuracy + 20 Hz mean rate cannot resolve OKN slow-phase direction reversal at the 88% Boly benchmark

GazeRecorder/GazeFlow.reason: Meets minimum 1 deg / 30 Hz; closed-source vendor binary; cloud-API latency ~30-60 ms is non-trivial vs <1 ms LSL but within OKN cycle (~300 ms) for switch-event classification

stack: GazeRecorder GazeFlow SDK (local Windows) OR GazeCloudAPI (browser, cross-platform)
Bistable plaid stimulus = HTML5 canvas + WebGL plaid renderer (gratings @ ~1 cyc/deg, 120 deg cross-angle, ~2 deg/s)
Sync: Lab Streaming Layer (LSL) marker stream from stimulus + gaze stream + OpenBCI Cyton EEG stream; align on shared LSL clock (sub-10 ms typical)
Post-hoc OKN classifier: vertical-edge optic-flow + state-machine sawtooth detector (Turuwhenua approach)
Validation: replay against research-grade Boly trace if available; require >=80% inter-rater agreement vs human OKN labeler before proceeding
integration_spec_if_viable.calibration_protocol: 9-point grid + drift correction every 60 s; reject trials with >2 deg post-hoc validation drift
risks: Webcam frame-rate jitter under load may drop below 30 Hz, corrupting OKN slope estimation
Head movement uncompensated — Boly used chinrest; webcam protocol must enforce equivalent head stabilization
GazeRecorder closed-source = reproducibility risk for publication; document SDK version + checksum
Cross-platform consistency: GazeFlow Windows-only; GazeCloudAPI browser path adds cloud latency

recommendation.primary: PROCEED with GazeRecorder/GazeCloudAPI webcam fallback at $0; reserve Pupil Labs purchase for replication/validation phase only if degraded webcam classifier fails to clear 75% accuracy on pilot N=5
rationale: Boly 2017 OKN-only decoder = 88% research-grade; webcam-OKN literature suggests 75-83% achievable with 1 deg / 30 Hz tools
$500 figure for Pupil Labs Core is OPTIMISTIC — actual MSRP is ~$2,000-$3,000 (verified pupil-labs.com); $500 likely DIY-frame + camera parts. Capex risk is 4-6x the cited budget.
$0 webcam route preserves pre-registration timeline; if pilot fails, escalate to Pupil Core purchase as a SECOND-stage decision with concrete failure data
Honest C3 gap: 5-13 percentage-point decoding accuracy loss is REAL and must be reported in paper Limitations; do NOT claim research-grade equivalence
recommendation.secondary_if_pilot_fails: Purchase Pupil Core (~$2-3k actual, not $500). Justification = OKN slow-phase velocity precision below sub-degree threshold cannot be achieved by any consumer webcam stack.
recommendation.budget_impact: $0 capex committed; $2-3k contingent escalation gated on pilot failure
